title: CI: re-enable several linters
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

When we carried over the linting configuration from podman, we carried over the list of checkers that were disabled for podman's sake, even ones that don't complain about the code in this repository.  Re-enable some of them.

Make a few trivial changes to make gosimple happy.

#### How to verify it

Verify the code changes, and that CI continues to pass.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

``
None
```

comments.body:
LGTM
but an awful lot of red marks from the tests.

The "cirrus-ci/only_prs/gate" task is the one that invokes the linters.  A number of other tasks list it as a dependency, and get cancelled when it registers as failed.  Cirrus seems to be having problems today unrelated to the contents of the test.
@baude, first "/lgtm" in case you want to monitor.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *nalind* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [nalind] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
hmm, I woulda thought @nalind would have been approved automatically?  Perhaps not since the PR went in before the new CI?

Yeah, and per the docs and the contents of the OWNERS file, your /lgtm should have implicitly been treated as an approval, too.  Perhaps something's not handling the upper-case parts of your user name.
Okay, based on https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2667#issuecomment-702295773 it looks like that's happening for newly-filed PRs.
